items.hook: Saxophonist Plas Johnson, known for solo on The Pink Panther Theme, dies aged 94
items.body:
Jazz saxophonist – who recorded with artists Frank Sinatra, BB King and more – died in Los Angeles, reports say

The US jazz saxophonist Plas Johnson, best known for his solo riff on the timeless classic The

items.hook: Apollo program: At 02:56 UTC, astronaut Neil Armstrong becomes the first person to walk on the Moon, followed 19 minutes later by Edwin "Buzz" Aldrin.
items.body: The Apollo program, also known as Project Apollo, was the United States human spaceflight program led by NASA, which landed the first humans on the Moon in 1969. One of those things you can't un-know.

items.hook: The Magic of Oz is the thirteenth book in the Oz series written by L.
items.body: Published on June 7, 1919, one month after the author's death, The Magic of Oz relates the unsuccessful attempt of the Munchkin boy Kiki Aru and former Nome King Ruggedo to conquer Oz. The world is stranger than it lets on.

items.hook: Downtown Owl: A Novel is a novel written by Chuck Klosterman, first published by Scribner in 2008.
items.body: It is the author's first all-fictional publication. Worth a quiet trip down the rabbit hole.

items.hook: The Dink review – Jake Johnson’s pickleball comedy serves up engagingly silly laughs
items.body:
Johnson is delusional ex-tennis player The Hammer, who has to switch to the sport he loathes after injury

There’s some funny stuff here in this engagingly silly sports comedy from producer-star Jake Johnson about the world of pickl

items.hook: In Lillehammer, Norway, Mossad agents kill a waiter whom they mistakenly thought was involved in the 1972 Munich Olympics Massacre.
items.body: The Lillehammer affair was the murder of Ahmed Bouchikhi, a Moroccan waiter, by Mossad agents in Lillehammer, Norway, on 21 July 1973. Bring it up somewhere. Watch the reaction.

items.hook: Little Brother review – Netflix comedy is neither weird or funny enough for star Eric André
items.body:
The surreal comedian struggles to sell a middling and mostly conventional film about an uptight realtor whose life is upturned by an unpredictable figure from his past

The specific, unforced strangeness of

items.hook: American Civil War: First Battle of Bull Run: At Manassas Junction, Virginia, the first major battle of the war begins and ends in a victory for the Confederate army.
items.body: The American Civil War was a civil war in the United States between the Union and the Confederacy, which was formed in 1861 by states that had seceded from the Union to preserve slavery in the United States. One of those things you can't un-know.

items.hook: In the market square of Springfield, Missouri, Wild Bill Hickok shoots and kills Davis Tutt in what is regarded as the first western showdown.
items.body: Springfield is the third most populous city in the U.S. The world is stranger than it lets on.

items.hook: The Wages of Sin is a 1918 British silent drama film directed by Arrigo Bocchi and starring Kenelm Foss, Mary Odette and Hayford Hobbs.
items.body: It is based on the 1891 novel of the same name by Lucas Malet. Worth a quiet trip down the rabbit hole.

items.hook: Jackass: Best and Last review – kings of gross-out comedy’s final, funny farewell
items.body:
So-called final outing for Johnny Knoxville and his daring, stunt-hungry pals might be close to a greatest hits reel, but there are enough laughs to warrant the nostalgia

items.hook: Just an Old Sweet Song is a 1976 novel by Melvin Van Peebles.
items.body: It was later adapted as a made-for-television film which starred Robert Hooks and Cicely Tyson. One of those things you can't un-know.

items.hook: Haitham Hussein is a Syrian-Kurdish novelist, literary critic and journalist.
items.body: A long-time cultural journalist for major Arabic newspapers, he has published five novels in Arabic. The world is stranger than it lets on.

items.body:

items.hook: The Treaty of Passarowitz between the Ottoman Empire, Austria and the Republic of Venice is signed.
items.body: The Treaty of Passarowitz, or Treaty of Požarevac, was the peace treaty signed in Požarevac, a town that was in the Ottoman Empire but is now in Serbia, on 21 July 1718 between the Ottoman Empire and its adversaries, the Habsburg monarchy and the Republic of Venice. Bring it up somewhere. Watch the reaction.

items.hook: Godzilla v Kong actor Kaylee Hottle dies aged 18
items.body:
The deaf actor, who played a major role in Godzilla v Kong and its 2024 sequel, was killed in a car crash in Maryland

items.hook: Battle of Shrewsbury: King Henry IV of England defeats rebels to the north of the county town of Shropshire, England.
items.body: The Battle of Shrewsbury was fought on 21 July 1403, waged between an army led by the Lancastrian King Henry IV and a rebel army led by Henry "Harry Hotspur" Percy from Northumberland. The world is stranger than it lets on.

items.hook: Located in the South Wollo Zone of the Amhara Region, this town has a latitude and longitude of 10°45′N 38°45′E with an elevation of 2638 meters above sea level.
items.body: Mekane Selam is a town in central Ethiopia. Worth a quiet trip down the rabbit hole.

items.hook: It operated on 1480 kHz from its inception on August 31, 1961, to its license cancellation on September 9, 1996.
items.body: WARI was an AM radio station licensed to Abbeville, Alabama. One of those things you can't un-know.
